annotation_basis: Blind annotation from source O&M text only.

concept_ground_truth.label: T1
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: On Helion HMC-500, record the complaint as chatter after roughing cast iron, toolholder sticking at the spindle nose, or dark contact marks found during tool change, and note the last operation, coolant condition, and affected holder. This review is focused on the BT40 taper mouth, drive dogs, and air-blast path rather than on the spindle cartridge generally.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T1 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T2
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Lock out spindle rotation and expose the taper mouth, the deeper taper zone, the drive dogs, the retention interface, the air-blast nozzle, and the lower drain groove without cleaning away the contamination pattern. Keep the upper nozzle and lower mouth visible together so chip entry and coolant pooling can be separated by direction.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T2 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T3
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Inspect the taper for chip pack, rust bloom, fretting dust, dried coolant, and holder witness at the 12, 3, 6, and 9 o'clock positions, then note whether the heaviest contamination sits at the lower mouth or deeper in the taper. A lower-mouth chip ring points to seating contamination, while deep taper staining pushes the boundary toward internal retention or purge failure.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T3 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T4
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Measure air-blast pressure at the nozzle and measure radial runout on a clean master arbor at the prescribed gauge line before any corrective cleaning is accepted. Normal runout with low purge pressure indicates a contamination path that starts at the nozzle or drain groove, while poor runout after cleaning pushes the investigation away from debris and toward taper or retention damage.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T4 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T5
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Compare mouth contact pattern with deeper taper contact after approved cleaning, then inspect the nozzle outlet and drain groove for packed fines that would route slurry downward into the seating area. If the runout stays normal and the contact pattern recovers once the lower mouth and nozzle are cleaned, isolate the fault boundary to chip-packed taper contamination and blocked purge path instead of to the spindle bearings.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T5 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T6
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Release the spindle only after the record names the contaminant type, the exact affected zone from nozzle to taper mouth to drive-dog area, and the measured recovery in purge pressure and master-arbor runout. The accepted condition is a clean taper that seats a verified holder without sticking and without renewed debris concentration at the lower mouth after the same coolant and tool-change cycle.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T6 retained verbatim from the source document.
